policy.title: RBI Issues Final Guidelines on Climate Risk Management for Scheduled Commercial Banks
policy.description: RBI released final guidelines requiring banks with assets above ₹10,000 crore to integrate climate risk into ICAAP and conduct annual climate stress tests by March 2027. Transition plans must be board-approved with interim 2030 targets for financed emissions.

policy.title: SEBI Expands BRSR Core to Top 500 Listed Companies with Assurance Mandate
policy.description: SEBI mandated BRSR Core reporting with reasonable assurance for top 500 companies by market cap from FY2026-27. Nine key indicators including Scope 1, 2 emissions and water intensity now require third-party verification.

policy.title: IRDAI Releases Sandbox Framework for Parametric Climate Insurance Products
policy.description: IRDAI announced a regulatory sandbox allowing insurers to pilot parametric products for flood, cyclone, and heat stress with automatic payouts triggered by IMD or satellite data. Pilots can run up to 24 months with relaxed capital requirements.

policy.title: Basel Committee Finalizes Climate Pillar 3 Disclosure Standards
policy.description: Basel Committee published final Pillar 3 climate disclosure framework requiring banks to report climate-related financial risks using standardized templates from January 2028. Includes mandatory transition risk metrics for high-carbon sector exposures.

policy.title: NGFS Releases Version 5 Scenarios with Enhanced Physical Risk Variables
policy.description: NGFS published updated climate scenarios with granular physical risk data for South Asia including district-level flood and heat projections. New scenarios incorporate 2025 NDC updates and feature 1.5°C overshoot pathways.

policy.title: EU Confirms CSRD Reporting Timelines for Non-EU Companies with Significant EU Revenue
policy.description: European Commission confirmed that non-EU companies with €150M+ EU revenue must comply with CSRD from FY2028. Indian multinationals with EU subsidiaries face consolidated sustainability reporting requirements under ESRS standards.

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence Partners with HDFC Bank for Physical Risk Assessment
competitors.description: Jupiter announced a multi-year partnership with HDFC Bank to provide ClimateScore Financial for portfolio-level physical risk assessment across commercial real estate and infrastructure lending. Deal reportedly covers 15,000+ asset locations.

competitors.title: Sprih Raises $12M Series A to Expand Carbon Accounting for Indian SMEs
competitors.description: Mumbai-based Sprih closed $12M Series A led by Lightspeed India to scale its automated carbon accounting platform. Company claims 200+ enterprise clients and plans to launch Scope 3 supply chain module targeting BRSR compliance.

competitors.title: Moody's Launches Physical Risk Score for Indian Banking Portfolios
competitors.description: Moody's ESG Solutions released India-specific physical climate risk scores covering 650 districts with hazard layers for flood, cyclone, drought, and heat stress. Product integrates with Moody's credit risk models for PD adjustments.

competitors.title: XDI Expands Gross Value at Risk Database to Cover India Infrastructure Assets
competitors.description: XDI added 2.3 million Indian infrastructure assets to its physical risk database covering power plants, ports, and road networks. Platform now provides asset-level damage probability under RCP 4.5 and 8.5 scenarios to 2100.

competitors.title: Watershed Partners with Tata Consultancy Services for Enterprise Decarbonization
competitors.description: Watershed announced strategic partnership with TCS to deliver carbon management solutions to large Indian enterprises. Integration will combine Watershed's emissions platform with TCS sustainability consulting for Fortune 500 India operations.

competitors.title: CarbonMint Launches Parametric Weather Index Platform for Agri-Lenders
competitors.description: CarbonMint released a weather index insurance platform targeting rural NBFCs and agri-finance companies. Product uses IMD rainfall data and NDVI satellite imagery for automatic claim triggers on crop loans.

research.title: Climate Risk and Bank Lending: Evidence from India's Flood-Prone Districts
research.description: RBI study finds banks with high exposure to flood-prone districts show 18% higher NPAs in agricultural portfolios during extreme monsoon years. Recommends climate-adjusted provisioning buffers of 50-100 bps for vulnerable sectors.

research.title: Scaling Parametric Insurance in South Asia: Opportunities and Barriers
research.description: Swiss Re estimates $8B parametric insurance opportunity in India by 2030, with agriculture and SME segments most underpenetrated. Report identifies basis risk and data quality as primary barriers, recommends public-private data partnerships.

research.title: ESG Data Gaps in Emerging Markets: Implications for Sustainable Finance
research.description: IFC analysis reveals 65% of Indian MSMEs lack standardized emissions data, creating $200B financing gap for sustainable lending. Recommends sector-specific emission factors and proxy methodologies for data-poor environments.

research.title: Physical Climate Risk Transmission to Credit Risk in Asian Banking Systems
research.description: BIS working paper models climate-credit risk transmission for Asian banks finding physical hazards could increase system-wide PDs by 2-4% under severe scenarios. India banking sector ranks third in regional vulnerability behind Philippines and Thailand.

research.title: Climate Scenario Analysis for Indian Financial Institutions: A Technical Guide
research.description: UNEP FI published India-specific guidance for climate scenario analysis covering sectoral pathways for power, steel, and cement. Includes transition risk methodology with carbon price assumptions aligned to India's net-zero 2070 target.

ourRead.headline: RBI's final climate guidelines create a hard deadline that will separate banks with credible risk intelligence from those scrambling for compliance.
body: This week's signals converge on a single theme: the institutionalization of climate risk in Indian finance is no longer aspirational—it's operational. Regulators have moved from consultation to mandate, global competitors are embedding themselves in Indian banking relationships, and research is producing actionable evidence on climate-credit transmission. The window for StepChange to establish dominance in Indian bank climate risk intelligence is narrowing rapidly.

The RBI's final climate risk guidelines (policy-1) are the most consequential development. March 2027 compliance for climate stress testing and ICAAP integration creates an 11-month runway for banks above ₹10,000 crore—roughly 40-50 institutions—to build or buy climate risk capabilities. The requirement for board-approved transition plans with 2030 interim targets means banks need both physical and transition risk modelling. StepChange should immediately prioritize sales conversations with Tier 2 PSU and private banks who lack internal climate teams and will need turnkey solutions.

Jupiter's HDFC partnership (comp-1) is a warning shot. A global player has landed a flagship Indian client, validating market demand but also establishing a beachhead. However, Jupiter's India data will rely heavily on global datasets downscaled to local conditions—StepChange's proprietary district-level hazard data and understanding of Indian monsoon dynamics is a differentiation point. The Moody's India launch (comp-3) is more concerning as they bundle physical risk with credit risk models banks already use. StepChange must articulate why specialized climate intelligence outperforms generalist ESG rating overlays.

The RBI's own research (research-1) finding 18% higher NPAs in flood-exposed districts is powerful ammunition for StepChange's sales narrative. Banks now have peer-reviewed evidence from their own regulator that physical risk drives credit losses. The recommended 50-100 bps provisioning buffer quantifies the cost of ignoring climate risk. StepChange should build this finding into every pitch deck and consider developing a provisioning optimization module that helps banks calibrate buffers using our hazard data.

Watch the IRDAI parametric sandbox (policy-3) closely—if pilot applications open in Q2, StepChange should partner with at least one insurer to demonstrate our hazard data as a trigger mechanism. Action item for this week: schedule briefings with the top 10 banks by asset size on RBI compliance timelines and secure two pilot commitments before Jupiter expands its India footprint.
